text: The positive effect of `treatment_olaparib` on `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `brca1_mutation` or `brca2_mutation` than in BRCA-wildtype patients.
rationale: It concerns olaparib and BRCA mutation status, not palbociclib with ER/HER2/PIK3CA/Ki67-defined benefit.

text: The effect of `treatment_pembrolizumab` on `pfs_months` is lower in patients with `autoimmune_disease` than in patients without autoimmune disease.
rationale: It concerns pembrolizumab and autoimmune disease with a lower effect, not the positive palbociclib effect in the specified subgroup.

text: The positive effect of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` on `pfs_months` is larger in `her2_low` patients than non-HER2-low patients.
rationale: It concerns sacituzumab govitecan and HER2-low status, not palbociclib benefit in ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 patients.

text: Higher `age_years` is associated with higher `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, labs, metastatic sites, and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns age_years and pfs_months, not palbociclib benefit within the ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype/low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: Patients with `stage_iv` have lower `pfs_months` than patients without `stage_iv` after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns stage_iv and pfs_months, not the specified subgroup-conditional palbociclib association.

text: Each one-point increase in `ecog_ps` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns ecog_ps and pfs_months, not treatment_palbociclib or the required biomarker-defined subgroup.

text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` have lower `pfs_months` than patients without brain metastases after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns brain metastases and pfs_months, not palbociclib response in the specified ER/HER2/PIK3CA/Ki67 subgroup.

text: Higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns albumin_g_dl and pfs_months, not the ground-truth palbociclib subgroup effect.

text: Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns weight_loss_pct_6mo and pfs_months, not palbociclib benefit in the specified subgroup.

text: Higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower `pfs_months` in an adjusted main-effects model.
rationale: Although ki67_pct is one subgroup-defining variable, the hypothesis tests only a Ki67 main effect and omits palbociclib and the full ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype subgroup.

text: Patients with `er_positive` have higher `pfs_months` than ER-negative patients in an adjusted main-effects model.
rationale: Although er_positive is one subgroup-defining variable, the hypothesis tests only an ER main effect and omits palbociclib and the full required subgroup.

text: Patients with `pik3ca_mutation` have lower `pfs_months` than PIK3CA-wildtype patients in an adjusted main-effects model.
rationale: Although pik3ca_mutation is one subgroup-defining variable, the hypothesis tests only a PIK3CA main effect and omits palbociclib and the full required subgroup.

text: Patients with `her2_positive` have lower `pfs_months` than HER2-negative patients after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: Although her2_positive is one subgroup-defining variable, the hypothesis tests only a HER2 main effect and omits palbociclib and the full required subgroup.

text: Patients receiving `treatment_palbociclib` have higher `pfs_months` than patients not receiving palbociclib in an adjusted main-effects model.
rationale: It tests a palbociclib main effect on pfs_months rather than the subgroup-conditional effect requiring ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 patients.

text: The positive effect of `treatment_tamoxifen` on `pfs_months` is larger in `er_positive` patients than ER-negative patients.
rationale: It uses tamoxifen rather than palbociclib and only considers ER status, so it is not the same data-generating association.

text: The positive effect of `treatment_palbociclib` on `pfs_months` is larger in `er_positive` patients than ER-negative patients.
rationale: It proposes ER-status heterogeneity for palbociclib but omits HER2 status, PIK3CA status, and the low-Ki67 subgroup condition.

text: Among patients with `er_positive` equal to 0, `treatment_palbociclib` does not increase `pfs_months` relative to no palbociclib.
rationale: It describes a lack of effect in ER-negative patients, which is only an implication outside the active subgroup and does not identify the full specified subgroup association.

text: The positive effect of `treatment_palbociclib` on `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `pik3ca_mutation` than in PIK3CA-wildtype patients.
rationale: It considers PIK3CA mutation as a modifier of palbociclib effect but omits ER positivity, HER2 negativity, and low Ki67 as required subgroup predicates.

text: Among `er_positive` and PIK3CA-wildtype patients, `treatment_palbociclib` increases `pfs_months` relative to no palbociclib.
rationale: It includes palbociclib, ER positivity, and PIK3CA-wildtype status but omits HER2-negative status and the Ki67 <= 14 low-proliferation condition.

text: Among `er_positive` patients with `pik3ca_mutation`, `treatment_palbociclib` does not increase `pfs_months` relative to no palbociclib.
rationale: It describes no palbociclib benefit in ER-positive PIK3CA-mutant patients, an inactive subgroup implication rather than the specified PIK3CA-wildtype low-Ki67 HER2-negative subgroup effect.

text: Higher `ki67_pct` reduces the positive effect of `treatment_palbociclib` on `pfs_months`.
rationale: It links Ki67 to palbociclib benefit directionally but omits the ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype subgroup definition and exact low-Ki67 condition.

text: Among `er_positive`, PIK3CA-wildtype patients with `ki67_pct` <= 25, `treatment_palbociclib` strongly increases `pfs_months`.
rationale: It is close but omits HER2-negative status and uses Ki67 <= 25 rather than the specified Ki67 <= 14 subgroup threshold.

text: Among `er_positive`, PIK3CA-wildtype patients with `ki67_pct` > 25, `treatment_palbociclib` does not increase `pfs_months`.
rationale: It describes no effect in a high-Ki67 subgroup, which is outside the active relationship and omits HER2-negative status and the exact specified active subgroup.

text: The positive effect of `treatment_palbociclib` on `pfs_months` is similar in `stage_iv` and non-stage-IV patients.
rationale: It uses the correct treatment and outcome but tests stage IV status rather than the ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: The positive effect of `treatment_trastuzumab` on `pfs_months` is larger in `her2_positive` patients than HER2-negative patients.
rationale: It concerns trastuzumab and HER2 status, not palbociclib in the specified multi-variable subgroup.

text: The positive effect of `treatment_olaparib` on `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `brca1_mutation` or `brca2_mutation` than in BRCA-wildtype patients.
rationale: It concerns olaparib and BRCA mutation status, not palbociclib with ER/HER2/PIK3CA/Ki67-defined benefit.

text: The effect of `treatment_pembrolizumab` on `pfs_months` is lower in patients with `autoimmune_disease` than in patients without autoimmune disease.
rationale: It concerns pembrolizumab and autoimmune disease with a lower effect, not the positive palbociclib effect in the specified subgroup.

text: The positive effect of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` on `pfs_months` is larger in `her2_low` patients than non-HER2-low patients.
rationale: It concerns sacituzumab govitecan and HER2-low status, not palbociclib benefit in ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 patients.

text: A 10-year higher `age_years` value is associated with higher mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for clinical stage, performance status, biomarkers, tumor burden, inflammatory markers, and treatment indicators.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns age_years and pfs_months, not palbociclib benefit within the ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype/low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: Patients with `ecog_ps` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `ecog_ps` set to 0 after adjustment for disease features, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns ecog_ps and pfs_months, not the specified treatment_palbociclib subgroup-conditional association.

text: Patients with `stage_iv` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` set to 0 after adjustment for other prognostic features and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns stage_iv and pfs_months, not palbociclib response in the defined biomarker subgroup.

text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without brain metastases, independent of stage, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns brain metastases and pfs_months, not the palbociclib effect conditioned on ER, HER2, PIK3CA, and Ki67 status.

text: Patients with `node_positive` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than node-negative patients after adjustment for stage, metastatic disease, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns node_positive and pfs_months, not the specified palbociclib subgroup effect.

text: Patients with `er_positive` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than ER-negative patients after adjustment for other receptor markers, disease severity, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis tests a main effect of er_positive on pfs_months rather than palbociclib increasing pfs_months only in the ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype/low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: After accounting for `er_positive`, patients with `pr_positive` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than PR-negative patients.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns pr_positive after accounting for ER status, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set or treatment effect.

text: Patients with `her2_positive` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than HER2-negative patients after adjustment for hormone receptors, other biomarkers, disease severity, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis tests a main effect of her2_positive on pfs_months, not a palbociclib benefit in HER2-negative patients within the full specified subgroup.

text: Patients with `her2_low` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients without HER2-low status after adjustment for `her2_positive`, hormone receptors, disease severity, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns her2_low and pfs_months, which is not the same as the specified palbociclib subgroup-conditional association.

text: Patients with `pik3ca_mutation` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `pik3ca_mutation` after adjustment for clinical severity, receptor markers, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This hypothesis tests a main adverse effect of pik3ca_mutation, whereas the ground truth is palbociclib increasing pfs_months in PIK3CA-wildtype patients within a broader biomarker-defined subgroup.

text: A 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for clinical severity, receptor markers, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This proposes a main association between ki67_pct and pfs_months, not a palbociclib benefit restricted to the ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype/low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: A 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, inflammatory markers, and treatments.
rationale: This concerns albumin_g_dl rather than treatment_palbociclib and the specified receptor/mutation/Ki67-defined subgroup.

text: A 5 percentage-point higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This concerns weight_loss_pct_6mo rather than the specified palbociclib subgroup-conditional effect on pfs_months.

text: A 100 U/L higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, other labs, and treatments.
rationale: This concerns ldh_u_l rather than the specified palbociclib effect active only in the defined biomarker subgroup.

text: After accounting for albumin, LDH, weight loss, stage, biomarkers, and treatments, higher `crp_mg_l` and higher `nlr` remain independently associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.
rationale: This concerns CRP and NLR main effects rather than treatment_palbociclib in the ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype/low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: Higher `tumor_size_cm` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for stage, nodal status, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
rationale: This concerns tumor_size_cm rather than the specified palbociclib subgroup-conditional association with pfs_months.

text: Patients receiving `treatment_palbociclib` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_palbociclib` after adjustment for clinical severity, biomarkers, labs, and other treatments.
rationale: Although it has the correct treatment, outcome, and positive direction, it proposes an overall palbociclib main effect rather than the defined subgroup-conditional effect.

text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `er_positive` set to 1 than in ER-negative patients.
rationale: This tests effect modification by ER status only and omits the required HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, and low-Ki67 subgroup predicates.

text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in tumors with high proliferation, measured as a 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct`.
rationale: This tests Ki67-related modification of palbociclib benefit but omits the required ER-positive, HER2-negative, and PIK3CA-wildtype subgroup conditions.

text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `pik3ca_mutation` set to 1 than in patients without `pik3ca_mutation`.
rationale: This tests PIK3CA-related modification of palbociclib benefit but omits the required ER-positive, HER2-negative, and low-Ki67 subgroup conditions.

text: The association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with `er_positive` set to 1.
rationale: It targets tamoxifen and ER status rather than palbociclib in the ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: The association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with `her2_positive` set to 1 or `her2_amplification` set to 1.
rationale: It targets trastuzumab and HER2-positive or HER2-amplified disease, not palbociclib benefit in HER2-negative, ER-positive, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 patients.

text: The association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with either `brca1_mutation` or `brca2_mutation` set to 1.
rationale: It targets olaparib and BRCA1/2 mutation status rather than palbociclib and the specified ER/HER2/PIK3CA/Ki67 subgroup.

text: The association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive`=0, `pr_positive`=0, and `her2_positive`=0).
rationale: It targets pembrolizumab in triple-negative patients, which differs from palbociclib in ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 disease.

text: The association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive`=0, `pr_positive`=0, and `her2_positive`=0).
rationale: It targets sacituzumab govitecan in triple-negative patients, not palbociclib benefit in the specified hormone-receptor-positive low-proliferation subgroup.

text: After adjustment for stage, brain metastases, biomarkers, labs, and treatments, additional metastatic-site indicators such as `visceral_mets` and common comorbidity indicators such as `diabetes_mellitus` and `hypertension` remain independently associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.

text: Patients with `er_positive` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `er_positive` equal to 0.
rationale: This tests only a main effect of ER positivity on pfs_months and omits palbociclib and the HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 subgroup condition.

text: Patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0.
rationale: This tests only a main effect of HER2 positivity on pfs_months rather than the palbociclib benefit in the ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0.
rationale: This concerns stage_iv, which is not one of the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 0.
rationale: This concerns brain metastases, which are not part of the ground-truth association.

text: Patients with `liver_mets` equal to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `liver_mets` equal to 0.

text: After adjustment for treatments and clinical severity variables, `er_positive` remains positively associated with `pfs_months`.

text: After adjustment for treatments and clinical severity variables, `her2_positive` remains negatively associated with `pfs_months`.
rationale: This proposes an adjusted main effect of HER2 positivity, not the specified palbociclib benefit in the HER2-negative subgroup with the other predicates.

text: Among patients with `er_positive` equal to 1, those with `treatment_palbociclib` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_palbociclib` equal to 0.
rationale: This includes ER-positive patients and palbociclib with the correct positive direction, but it omits the required HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, and low-Ki67 subgroup restrictions.

text: Among patients with `er_positive` equal to 1, those with `treatment_tamoxifen` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_tamoxifen` equal to 0.

text: Among patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1, those with `treatment_trastuzumab` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_trastuzumab` equal to 0.
rationale: This concerns trastuzumab in HER2-positive patients, whereas the ground truth is palbociclib benefit in HER2-negative, ER-positive, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 patients.

text: Among patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0, `treatment_trastuzumab` has little or no positive association with mean `pfs_months`.
rationale: It concerns trastuzumab in HER2-negative patients, not palbociclib in the ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: Among patients with `brca1_mutation` equal to 1 or `brca2_mutation` equal to 1, those with `treatment_olaparib` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_olaparib` equal to 0.
rationale: It concerns olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated patients rather than palbociclib benefit in the specified ER/HER2/PIK3CA/Ki67-defined subgroup.

text: Among patients with both `brca1_mutation` and `brca2_mutation` equal to 0, `treatment_olaparib` has little or no positive association with mean `pfs_months`.
rationale: It concerns lack of olaparib benefit in BRCA-wildtype patients, not the specified palbociclib subgroup association with pfs_months.

text: Among patients with `autoimmune_disease` equal to 0, those with `treatment_pembrolizumab` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_pembrolizumab` equal to 0.
rationale: It concerns pembrolizumab among patients without autoimmune disease, not palbociclib in the specified biomarker subgroup.

text: Among patients with `autoimmune_disease` equal to 1, the positive association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and mean `pfs_months` is attenuated compared with patients without autoimmune disease.
rationale: It concerns autoimmune disease modifying pembrolizumab benefit, which uses different variables and treatment from the ground-truth association.

text: Among patients with high inflammatory burden, defined as `crp_mg_l` above the 75th percentile or `nlr` above the 75th percentile, `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` equal to 1 is associated with higher mean `pfs_months` than no sacituzumab govitecan.
rationale: It concerns sacituzumab govitecan in a high inflammatory burden subgroup, not palbociclib in the ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: The positive association between `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` and mean `pfs_months` is larger in the high inflammatory burden subgroup than in the lower inflammatory burden subgroup.
rationale: It proposes inflammatory burden effect modification of sacituzumab govitecan, not the specified palbociclib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: The positive association between `treatment_palbociclib` and mean `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `ki67_pct` in the highest quartile than among patients with lower `ki67_pct`.
rationale: Although it involves palbociclib, Ki67, and pfs_months with a compatible low-versus-high proliferation direction, it omits the required ER-positive, HER2-negative, and PIK3CA-wildtype subgroup predicates.

text: After adjustment for disease severity and treatments, a one-standard-deviation increase in `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher `pfs_months`.

text: After adjustment for disease severity and treatments, a one-standard-deviation increase in `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower `pfs_months`.

text: The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is strongest when both `her2_positive` and `her2_amplification` equal 1, producing a positive three-way interaction among `treatment_trastuzumab`, `her2_positive`, and `her2_amplification`.

text: The positive association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is larger when `pr_positive` equals 1, even after accounting for `er_positive` and disease severity.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns tamoxifen and PR positivity rather than palbociclib and the specified ER/HER2/PIK3CA/Ki67-defined subgroup.

text: The positive association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `bone_mets` equal to 1 than among patients with `bone_mets` equal to 0.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns sacituzumab govitecan and bone metastases, not the specified palbociclib subgroup effect on pfs_months.

text: The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is attenuated among patients with `chronic_kidney_disease` equal to 1 after accounting for BRCA mutation status and severity.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns olaparib and chronic kidney disease rather than palbociclib in the specified biomarker-defined subgroup.

text: Among patients not fully captured by `her2_positive`, `her2_low` modifies the association between `treatment_trastuzumab` and `pfs_months`, with a positive `treatment_trastuzumab` by `her2_low` interaction after adjustment.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns trastuzumab and HER2-low status, not palbociclib in ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 disease.

text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is smaller as `ecog_ps` increases, yielding a negative `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `ecog_ps` interaction.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns pembrolizumab effect modification by ECOG performance status, not the specified palbociclib subgroup association.

text: The association between `treatment_tamoxifen` and `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `postmenopausal` equal to 1 than in patients with `postmenopausal` equal to 0 after accounting for hormone receptor status and severity.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns tamoxifen and postmenopausal status rather than palbociclib and the ER/HER2/PIK3CA/Ki67 subgroup.

text: `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 1 is associated with shorter `pfs_months`, and this negative association is partly offset among patients with `treatment_palbociclib` equal to 1.
rationale: Although it includes palbociclib and PIK3CA, it posits an effect involving PIK3CA-mutated patients and omits the required ER-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: The negative association of `stage_iv` with `pfs_months` is more severe when `liver_mets` equals 1, yielding a negative `stage_iv` by `liver_mets` interaction after adjustment.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns stage IV disease and liver metastases rather than treatment_palbociclib and the specified biomarker-defined subgroup.

text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is attenuated among patients with `interstitial_lung_disease_history` equal to 1.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns pembrolizumab and interstitial lung disease history, not the specified palbociclib subgroup effect.

text: Patients with `hemoglobin_g_dl` in the lowest quartile have lower `pfs_months`, and this negative association is not fully rescued by `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan`.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns hemoglobin and sacituzumab govitecan rather than palbociclib in the ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype/low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: Older age, measured per 10-year increase in `age_years`, is associated with shorter `pfs_months`, and the association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` does not materially increase with age.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns age and tamoxifen rather than the specified palbociclib subgroup-conditional effect on pfs_months.

text: `venous_thromboembolism_history` equal to 1 is associated with shorter `pfs_months`, especially among patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1.
rationale: This hypothesis concerns venous thromboembolism and stage IV status rather than the specified palbociclib biomarker-defined subgroup.

text: Adding biomarker-matched treatment interactions (`treatment_palbociclib` by `er_positive`, `treatment_tamoxifen` by `er_positive`, `treatment_trastuzumab` by `her2_positive`, and `treatment_olaparib` by any BRCA1/2 mutation) improves prediction of `pfs_months` beyond main effects and severity covariates.
rationale: Although it includes a palbociclib-by-ER-positive interaction for pfs_months, it omits the required HER2-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype, and low-Ki67 subgroup predicates that define the ground-truth association.

text: Patients with `stage_iv=1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv=0`.
rationale: This tests stage IV status against pfs_months, not palbociclib benefit in the ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype/low-Ki67 subgroup.

text: Patients with `has_brain_mets=1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `has_brain_mets=0`.
rationale: This concerns brain metastases and pfs_months, which are not the ground-truth treatment and subgroup variables.

text: Each 1-point increase in `ecog_ps` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease, biomarker, laboratory, and treatment variables.
rationale: This proposes an ECOG performance-status main effect, not the subgroup-conditional palbociclib association with pfs_months.

text: Each additional year of `age_years` is associated with higher `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease, biomarker, laboratory, and treatment variables.
rationale: This proposes an age main effect, not the specified palbociclib effect within the biomarker-defined subgroup.

text: Higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher `pfs_months` after adjustment.
rationale: This tests albumin as a predictor of pfs_months rather than treatment_palbociclib in the specified ER/HER2/PIK3CA/Ki67 subgroup.

text: Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment.
rationale: This tests weight loss as a predictor of pfs_months and does not involve palbociclib or the required subgroup predicates.

text: Higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment.

text: Patients with `egfr_mutation=1` have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `egfr_mutation=0`, independent of other biomarkers and baseline factors.

text: Patients with `tmb_high` have lower `pfs_months` than patients without `tmb_high` after adjustment for clinical features, biomarkers, and treatments.

text: Patients with four or more adverse factors among `mcrpc`, `psa_ng_ml>=41.28`, `ecog_ps=2`, `weight_loss_pct_6mo>=6.4`, `crp_mg_l>=6.9825`, `albumin_g_dl<=3.5`, `ar_v7_positive=1`, and `brca2_mutation=1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with fewer than four adverse factors.
rationale: Although it includes some relevant markers, it proposes an adverse-factor count effect and omits treatment_enzalutamide and msi_high, so it is not the same data-generating process.

text: In an adjusted linear probability model for `objective_response`, `treatment_enzalutamide` remains positively associated with response in the reference subgroup, while the `treatment_enzalutamide` interactions with `mcrpc`, `ar_v7_positive`, and `brca2_mutation` remain negative.
rationale: This captures a positive enzalutamide effect conditioned on some relevant reference factors, but it omits msi_high/MSI-stable status, which is required for the ground-truth subgroup.
